question: Which Spring Mail class supports HTML content, attachments, and inline images?
options: SimpleMailMessage — it handles all email formats
MimeMessage configured via MimeMessageHelper — multipart mode required
HtmlMailMessage — the Spring Boot HTML-specific class
MailMessage with setHtmlContent() method

question: Why should SMTP credentials be configured as ${MAIL_USERNAME} in application.yml rather than as literal strings?
options: Spring Boot can only read SMTP credentials from environment variables
Literal strings in application.yml are encrypted by Spring, but ${} values are not
Hardcoding credentials in source files risks committing secrets to version control
Gmail requires environment variable format for authentication to succeed

question: What is the second boolean argument to helper.setText(content, true) in MimeMessageHelper?
options: true = send asynchronously; false = send synchronously
true = content is HTML; false = content is plain text
true = include a read receipt; false = no read receipt
true = attach the text as a file; false = embed in body

question: You add @Async to sendWelcomeEmail() but it runs synchronously. What is the most likely cause?
options: @Async requires the method to return CompletableFuture to work
@EnableAsync was not added to a @Configuration class
SimpleMailMessage does not support asynchronous sending
@Async only works on methods annotated with @Transactional

question: Which approach is recommended for integration-testing email sending without a real SMTP server?
options: Use @SpringBootTest with a real Gmail account in test application.yml
Disable email sending in tests using spring.mail.enabled=false
Use GreenMail — an in-memory SMTP server that captures sent messages for assertion
Mock the JavaMailSender and verify the exact MIME bytes sent
